alerts.title: Khamenei reportedly cut off from direct contact amid US strike fears
alerts.summary: CBS reporting (via arabnews.jp) indicates Iran's Supreme Leader has been isolated from direct contact over fears of a US decapitation strike, even as NYT reports Trump is actively weighing options for new strikes against Iran in parallel with negotiations. The threat level remains at 9/10 with breakout still estimated at 1-2 weeks; combined signals suggest Washington is preserving a kinetic option despite Rubio's diplomatic tone.

alerts.title: Russia reportedly fires two Oreshnik IRBMs in single night against Ukraine
alerts.summary: Rybar (~116K views) reports that for the first time since the start of the SVO, Russian forces conducted two Oreshnik intermediate-range ballistic missile launches in a single night, striking targets in Bila Tserkva and frontline Druzhkivka alongside Tsirkon strikes on Kyiv. Combined Telegram engagement across Rybar posts exceeds 500K views, with DeepState UA confirming one of the largest sustained overnight attacks on Kyiv.

alerts.title: Rubio signals 'solid' Iran deal could land Monday as GOP hawks revolt
alerts.summary: BBC reports Secretary of State Rubio said a 'solid' US-Iran deal may be announced Monday, providing the first specific timeline. Middle East Eye reports Republican hawks are publicly attacking the emerging framework as a 'nightmare for Israel,' and NYT notes Netanyahu has been reduced from 'co-pilot' to 'passenger' in Trump's Iran strategy — pointing to a domestic political fight regardless of outcome.

alerts.title: Iran's Larestan underground missile base reportedly back in operation
alerts.summary: Middle East Spectator (~44K views) reports the entrance to Iran's Larestan underground missile complex in southern Iran near the Persian Gulf has been repaired and the facility is again operational. The reconstitution of a strike-capable base within range of Gulf shipping lanes comes as US deploys an 'umbrella' of defense and tech assets to shield Strait of Hormuz traffic, per DefenseScoop.

alerts.title: Israeli strikes kill medics in Lebanon as fragile truce frays further
alerts.summary: NYT and The Guardian report fresh Israeli bombardment of southern Lebanon killed medics and reduced buildings to craters, expanding on yesterday's Beirut strike. IDF is maintaining high alert and only modestly demobilizing reservists despite a potential US-Iran deal, per The Jerusalem Post, suggesting the northern front remains a likely flashpoint regardless of the Iran track.

analysis_note: US-Iran diplomacy and military escalation are running on parallel tracks: Rubio floats a Monday deal while Trump weighs strikes and Khamenei reportedly goes into hiding, even as Iran rebuilds a Gulf missile base and Israel keeps the Lebanon front hot.
